questions.text: Plants which grow on saline soils are
questions.solution:
✅ **Correct Answer: Halophytes (C)**

Halophytes are plants specially adapted to grow in soils with high salt concentration (e.g., mangroves, Suaeda). They have salt-excreting glands, succulence and vivipary. In DRDO CEPTAM-11 General Science, this is a high-weightage topic under plant adaptations and economic botany because India has a vast coastline (7,500+ km) where DRDO uses halophytes for coastal bio-shields, erosion control, cyclone protection and soil stabilisation near naval bases and forward posts.

**Option-wise Analysis:**
• **Xerophytes (A):** Adapted to dry/arid conditions (deep roots, reduced leaves) — not for saline waterlogging.
• **Hydrophytes (B):** Live in water (aerenchyma, reduced cuticle) — cannot tolerate high salt.
• **Succulents (D):** Store water in fleshy tissues (e.g., cactus) — not specifically for salinity.

**Important Exam Concept:** Plant adaptations in extreme environments + defence applications in coastal warfare and environmental engineering (frequently repeated in DRDO papers).

questions.text: Epiphytes are plants which depend on other plants for
questions.solution:
✅ **Correct Answer: Mechanical support (B)**

Epiphytes (e.g., orchids, mosses) grow on trees for physical support only; they absorb moisture and nutrients from air/rain. DRDO relevance: biomimicry for lightweight camouflage netting, UAV sensor mounting and forest concealment studies in Northeast border areas.

**Option-wise Analysis:**
• **Food (A):** Wrong — epiphytes are not parasitic.
• **Shade (C):** Incidental, not the primary dependence.
• **Water (D):** Absorbed from atmosphere, not host.

**Important Exam Concept:** Symbiotic relationships & biomimicry for defence technology (high-weightage in taxonomy & applied biology).

questions.text: The maximum amount of water, which plants need to grow that is absorbed by the following?
questions.solution:
✅ **Correct Answer: Root hairs (D)**

Root hairs (extensions of epidermal cells) massively increase surface area (10–100 times) for passive water absorption by osmosis. DRDO context: critical for soldier hydration strategies in desert operations and hydroponics-based forward-area ration production.

**Option-wise Analysis:**
• **Embryonic zone (A):** Meristematic region — no absorption role.
• **Growing point (B):** Apical meristem — growth, not absorption.
• **Zone of elongation (C):** Cell expansion zone — not primary absorption site.

**Important Exam Concept:** Plant physiology & water relations in extreme environments (repeated in DRDO papers).

questions.text: A hydrophyte is
questions.solution:
✅ **Correct Answer: An aquatic plant (B)**

Hydrophytes (e.g., Hydrilla, lotus) live in water with aerenchyma, reduced cuticle and air sacs. DRDO applications: water purification systems for naval bases and aquatic camouflage studies.

**Option-wise Analysis:**
• **A marine animal (A):** Wrong kingdom.
• **A plant disease (C):** Unrelated.
• **A rootless plant (D):** Some hydrophytes have roots; not the defining feature.

**Important Exam Concept:** Aquatic adaptations & ecosystem management (naval & border ecology).

questions.text: The radiant energy absorbed by the plants results in
questions.solution:
✅ **Correct Answer: Photolysis of water (A)**

In light reaction (PS-II), radiant energy splits water: 2H₂O → 4H⁺ + 4e⁻ + O₂. DRDO relevance: oxygen generation for submarine life-support and bio-regenerative air revitalisation systems.

**Option-wise Analysis:**
• **Oxidation of chlorophyll (B):** Not primary result.
• **Reduction of oxygen (C):** Occurs in respiration.
• **Oxidation of carbon dioxide (D):** Reverse of photosynthesis.

**Important Exam Concept:** Light reaction & oxygen evolution (core photosynthesis for DRDO environmental science).

questions.text: Which of the following is not correctly matched?
questions.solution:
✅ **Correct Answer: Herbivore - Secondary consumer (D)**

Herbivores are primary consumers. DRDO context: understanding trophic levels for food security modelling in high-altitude border posts and bioenergy planning.

**Option-wise Analysis:**
• **Autotroph - Producer (A):** Correct.
• **Heterotroph - Consumer (B):** Correct.
• **Saprothroph - Decomposer (C):** Correct.

**Important Exam Concept:** Trophic levels & energy pyramids (ecology for defence sustainability).

questions.text: Trophic level-I includes
questions.solution:
✅ **Correct Answer: Green plants (D)**

Producers (autotrophs) form trophic level-I. DRDO relevance: energy flow analysis for sustainable ration supply in remote military camps.

**Option-wise Analysis:**
• **Herbivorous animals (A):** Primary consumers (level-II).
• **Carnivorous animals (B):** Secondary/tertiary consumers.
• **Omnivorous animals (C):** Multiple levels.

**Important Exam Concept:** Trophic levels & energy pyramids (ecology for defence sustainability).

questions.text: A group of archaebacteria is used in the production of
questions.solution:
✅ **Correct Answer: Methane (B)**

Methanogens produce CH₄ in biogas plants. DRDO uses this for clean energy in remote military camps and waste management.

**Option-wise Analysis:**
• Others not produced by archaebacteria in this context.

**Important Exam Concept:** Extremophiles & renewable energy for field operations.

questions.text: How do most insects respire?
questions.solution:
✅ **Correct Answer: By tracheal system (D)**

Direct diffusion via spiracles and tracheoles. DRDO biomimicry: micro-air channels in lightweight soldier gear and UAV cooling systems.

**Option-wise Analysis:**
• **Through skin (A):** Earthworms.
• **Through gills (B):** Aquatic insects.
• **By lungs (C):** Vertebrates.

**Important Exam Concept:** Respiratory adaptations & bio-inspired engineering.

questions.text: Fishes survive under the frozen lake, because
options: Fishes are in hibernation under it
Water does not freeze near bottom
Ice is the conductor of heat
questions.solution:
✅ **Correct Answer: Water does not freeze near bottom (C)**

Ice floats; bottom water stays at 4°C (maximum density). DRDO naval research uses this for cold-water diving physiology and submarine operations.

**Option-wise Analysis:**
• Others factually incorrect.

**Important Exam Concept:** Anomalous water expansion & aquatic survival (naval ops).

questions.text: After bringing out of water fishes die because
options: They get excess quantity of oxygen
questions.solution:
✅ **Correct Answer: They cannot breathe (C)**

Fishes use gills for dissolved oxygen; air has no dissolved O₂ for them. DRDO relevance: diver training and naval rescue physiology.

**Option-wise Analysis:**
• Others incorrect or secondary.

**Important Exam Concept:** Aquatic respiration & naval technology.

questions.text: Fish generally breathe through
questions.solution:
✅ **Correct Answer: Gills (C)**

Counter-current exchange extracts dissolved O₂. DRDO: gill-mimicry in underwater breathing apparatus and sonar studies.

**Option-wise Analysis:**
• Others not respiratory organs.

**Important Exam Concept:** Aquatic respiration & naval technology.

questions.text: Redness in apple is due to
questions.solution:
✅ **Correct Answer: Anthocyanin (A)**

pH-dependent flavonoid pigment responsible for red colour. DRDO: natural food colourants for troop rations and packaging.

**Option-wise Analysis:**
• Lycopene (B): Tomato. Carotene (C): Carrot. Xanthophyll (D): Yellow pigments.

**Important Exam Concept:** Plant pigments & nutritional security.

questions.text: Red colour in tomato is due to
questions.solution:
✅ **Correct Answer: Lycopene (B)**

Carotenoid antioxidant giving red colour. DRDO: antioxidant-rich supplements for soldiers.

**Option-wise Analysis:**
• Capsaicin (A): Chillies. Xanthophyll (C): Yellow pigments.

**Important Exam Concept:** Carotenoids & health in defence rations.

questions.text: The yellow colour of papaya is due to
questions.solution:
✅ **Correct Answer: Caricaxanthin (C)**

Specific xanthophyll pigment. DRDO: natural colourants for packaged foods and rations.

**Option-wise Analysis:**
• Papain (A): Enzyme. Lycopene (B): Red. Carotene (D): Orange.

**Important Exam Concept:** Fruit pigments & economic botany.

questions.text: Opium is obtained from which part of the poppy plant?
questions.solution:
✅ **Correct Answer: Unripe fruits (C)**

Latex from incisions on green capsules (unripe fruits). DRDO medical research: controlled alkaloids for pain management in field hospitals.

**Option-wise Analysis:**
• Flower/Leaves/Roots (A,B,D): No commercial opium.

**Important Exam Concept:** Economic botany & pharmacognosy (defence medicine).

questions.text: Which of the following groups of living beings belong to the same species?
options: Chinese, American, Indians and Black Africans
questions.solution:
✅ **Correct Answer: Chinese, American, Indians and Black Africans (A)**

All are Homo sapiens. DRDO human factors research: uniform physiological/ergonomic standards for diverse troops.

**Option-wise Analysis:**
• Others are different species.

**Important Exam Concept:** Biological species concept & human evolution.

questions.text: Yeast is an example of
questions.solution:
✅ **Correct Answer: Fungus (B)**

Unicellular ascomycete fungus. DRDO: fermentation for biofuel, food preservation and ration production.

**Option-wise Analysis:**
• Others wrong kingdom.

**Important Exam Concept:** Fungi classification & industrial microbiology.

questions.text: Yeast and mushrooms are
questions.solution:
✅ **Correct Answer: Fungi (C)**

Heterotrophic, chitinous cell wall. DRDO: mushroom cultivation for protein-rich soldier diet and mycorrhizal biofertilizers.

**Option-wise Analysis:**
• Others wrong group.

**Important Exam Concept:** Kingdom Fungi & applied mycology.

questions.text: What is a mushroom?
questions.solution:
✅ **Correct Answer: Fungi (A)**

Basidiomycete fruiting body. DRDO: protein source for high-altitude rations.

**Option-wise Analysis:**
• Others incorrect.

**Important Exam Concept:** Edible fungi & nutritional security.
